name: SCLEROSING MESENTERITIS
definition: CHRONIC FIBROSING MESENTERITIS = MULTIFOCAL SUBPERITONEAL SCLEROSIS = SCLEROSING rare idiopathic disorder characterized by tumorlike masses in mesentery composed of chronic degeneration and necrosis of mesenteric fat predominantly chronic inflammatory stage of mesenteritis predominantly fibrotic stage of mesenteritis

demographics.Path: solitary / multifocal well-demarcated / diffusely infiltrating (mesenteric thickening)
demographics.Histo: chronic mesenteric inflammation with lipid-laden macrophages (= lipophages) +
demographics.Location: root of small bowel mesentery, colonic mesocolon

distribution: root of small bowel mesentery, colonic mesocolon
discriminating_features: SBO: intestinal dilatation, fluid levels, fluid-filled bowel
compression / distortion of duodenum near ligament of Treitz
separation of small bowel loops with fixation + kinking + angulation ← shortening +

general: SBO: intestinal dilatation, fluid levels, fluid-filled bowel
compression / distortion of duodenum near ligament of Treitz
separation of small bowel loops with fixation + kinking + angulation ← shortening +
irregular fold thickening of small bowel ← reactive mural thickening
CT: multiple cystic masses in mesentery (= loose myxomatous composition / lymphatic
tumoral pseudocapsule = peripheral band of soft-tissue attenuation at the edge of
± small < 5 mm lymph nodes within lesion
"fat ring" sign = preservation of fat as a halo of low attenuation surrounding nondisplaced
mesenteric thickening with fine stellate pattern (= radiating strands of fibrosis) extending to
retraction of small bowel loops (= desmoplastic response)
US: heterogeneous mass with hypo- and hyperechoic features
MR: low to intermediate SI on T1WI + very low signal on T2WI
high SI on T2WI (= predominant myxomatous change)
narrowing of bowel loop with spiculations → intestinal obstruction

clinical.Prognosis: usually benign self-limited course with complete spontaneous resolution
clinical.Cx: compression of mesenteric vessels + formation of collateral vessels ± small bowel
